name: Caruru De Camarao
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Okra, fresh or frozen (10 ounces)
1/3 cup Olive oil or Spanish oil
2 tablespoons Coriander, fresh (or dried if unavailable)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a 12-inch skillet over high heat.
2. Add shrimp and cook, stirring constantly, until opaque and tender, about 3 minutes.
3. Remove shrimp with a slotted spoon to a platter; set aside. Reduce heat to medium-low.
4. Sauté onion in the same skillet, stirring frequently, until soft, about 5 minutes.
5. Add green pepper, tomatoes, okra, and ground dried shrimp (if using). Stir gently.
6. Pour boiling water over coconut; stir to combine with manioc meal (or use 2 tablespoons plain bread crumbs).
7. Mix the mixture into the tomato sauce. Cover and simmer on low heat until okra is tender, about 25 minutes.
8. Add reserved shrimp to the tomato sauce and cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until hot.
9. Stir in olive oil, ground peanuts, and coriander. Season to taste with salt and white pepper.
